<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed embedding model health check by adding mode metadata to LiteLLM config</title>
  <subtitle>nomic-embed-text now marked as embedding type to prevent incorrect health check failures</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added model_info.mode=embedding to nomic-embed-text configuration in /home/nugad/.claude/litellm_config.yaml</fact>
    <fact>Embedding models do not support generate/completion endpoints used by default health checks</fact>
    <fact>LiteLLM uses mode metadata to apply appropriate health check methods per model type</fact>
    <fact>Configuration prevents "does not support generate" errors for embedding models</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed the unhealthy endpoint issue discovered in health checks by adding proper model type metadata to the LiteLLM configuration. The nomic-embed-text model was failing health checks because LiteLLM was attempting to test it using the generate endpoint, which embedding models don't support. By adding `model_info: mode: embedding` to the model configuration, LiteLLM now knows to use embedding-specific health checks instead. This resolves the 8:1 healthy/unhealthy ratio and ensures the embedding model is properly monitored. The fix follows LiteLLM's configuration schema for distinguishing between completion models and embedding models.</narrative>

  </concepts>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
Two things revealed here:

1. The sentinel agent server process is running (ps aux shows it)
2. The raw claw output format was checked with --output-format json

The key finding is the actual claw JSON output structure:
```json
{
  "iterations": 1,
  "message": "{ \"name\": \"StructuredOutput\", \"arguments\": { \"message\": \"hello\" } }",
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  "tool_results": [],
  "tool_uses": [],
  "usage": {...}
}
```

So claw's JSON format has:
- The actual response is in the "message" field
- Inside that message is ANOTHER JSON string containing the StructuredOutput tool call
- The parser needs to handle this nested JSON structure

This means the current parser might not be correctly handling claw's --output-format json, which wraps everything in an outer JSON object with a "message" field that contains a stringified JSON tool call.

This is a discovery about the actual format claw uses.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Claw JSON output format uses nested structure with stringified tool calls in message field</title>
  <subtitle>Raw claw output wraps StructuredOutput tool call as JSON string inside outer response object</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Claw --output-format json returns structured object with iterations, message, model, tool_results, tool_uses, and usage fields</fact>
    <fact>Actual AI response content is in the message field as a stringified JSON object</fact>
    <fact>Message contains nested StructuredOutput tool call with name and arguments</fact>
    <fact>The arguments.message field contains the final text response</fact>
    <fact>Current parser expects line-by-line JSON, not this nested wrapper format</fact>
    <fact>Sentinel agent server still running from previous start command</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Examined claw's actual JSON output format to understand parsing requirements. When using --output-format json, claw returns a structured response object where the AI's output is nested as a JSON string within the "message" field. This message contains a StructuredOutput tool call with the actual text in arguments.message, creating a double-nesting structure: outer JSON → message string → inner JSON → arguments → final text. The format also includes metadata like iteration count, model name, and token usage. The current _parse_claw_output implementation expects line-by-line SendUserMessage tool calls in text format, not this wrapped JSON structure, explaining why the sentinel agent returns unparsed output. The parser needs adjustment to handle both the --output-format text mode (which outputs line-by-line tool calls) and potentially the json mode with its nested structure.</narrative>

    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_modified>
</observation>
